plain_definition: VARA's category, under Dubai's Virtual Asset Issuance Rulebook, for a virtual asset designed to maintain a stable value by referencing real-world assets, which VARA states includes but is not limited to FIAT. A currency-referencing ART is the narrower FRVA subset, covered by the Rulebook's own dedicated annex. This is VARA's own regime-specific term and a different concept from the European Union's MiCA 'asset-referenced token' (see asset-referenced-token), which this glossary tracks as a separate entry rather than merging the two.
formal_definition: VARA's Virtual Asset (VA) Issuance Rulebook (September 2023 edition) introduces two categories of VA issuance for an ART, determining whether an issuer requires Registration with VARA pre-approval or a Full-fledged Regulated VARA Licence prior to issuance, alongside whitepaper/public-disclosure, issuer-compliance, supervision, examination and enforcement requirements. VARA describes this as the first-of-its-kind regulatory position on issuance of specific categories of Virtual Assets backed by Real World Assets.
